title: KOLASIB SP IN NH-06 BILKHAWTHLIR LEH KOLASIB INKARA KAWNG SIAM CHHUNG LIRTHEI LIAN (HMV) TLAN DAN TUR RUAHMANNA A SIAM
content:
446/2026-2027

Kolasib, 9th May, 2026:Kolasib Superintendent of Police Pu Lalrinpuia Varte chuan Mizoram State Sawrkar Notification leh Motor Vehicle Act 1988-in thuneihna a pek angin, NHIDCL hnuaia contractor ten National Highway 06 (Bilkhawthlir 70+000 KM leh Kolasib 95+000 KM inkar) a kawngpui siamthat hna chak zawka an thawh theih nan, a hnuaia mi ang hian lirthei lian (Heavy Motor Vehicle - HMV) te tlan dan tur thupek a chhuah.

He thupek ang hian May ni 11, 2026 atanga May ni 20, 2026 chhungin Bilkhawthlir (Kharzawl) leh Hmawngkhawthlir, Kolasib inkarah lirthei lian (HMV) te tan a hnuaia hun tarlan ang hian kawng khar leh hawn a ni ang:

Kawngpui khar hun (Road Closure Hours):

10:00 AM atanga 1:00 PM

2:00 PM atanga 5:00 PM

6:00 PM atanga 9:00 PM

Kawngpui hawn hun (Road Opening Hours):

1:00 PM atanga 2:00 PM

5:00 PM atanga 6:00 PM

9:00 PM atanga 10:00 AM

Kawng khar (Road Block) hun chhung hian Vairengte atanga lo chho lirthei lian (HMV) te chu Dochhuma hmunah dan an ni ang a, Silchar lam pan lirthei lian (HMV) te chu Hmawngkhawthlir-ah dan an ni thung ang.

He thuchhuah chungchanga harsatna neite chuan IC Bilkhawthlir OP (Ph.No. 9862316186) leh IC Traffic Cell, Kolasib (Ph. No. 9862306914) ah te biak theih reng an ni a. He thupek zawm lo chu Section 179 MV Act hmanga hrem an ni ang.

title: HEALTH MINISTER PI LALRINPUII GRACES MIZORAM'S FIRST NCC FEST & PARASAILING CAMP IN LUNGLEI
content:
66/2026-2027

Lunglei, 9 May 2026:Health & Family Welfare Minister Pi Lalrinpuii today graced the ongoing first-ever NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp in Mizoram, organised by 2 Mizo Battalion National Cadet Corps at C. Ṭhuamluaia Mual, Lunglei, as the chief guest.

The Minister was accompanied by Lunglei High Powered Committee (HPC) Vice Chairman Pu V. Malsawmtluanga, Lunglei Municipal Council (LMC) Vice Chairman Pu K. Lalrinawma and district officials. Commanding Officer of 2 Mizo Battalion NCC, Col. Sagar K. Mohanty, along with NCC officers, army personnel and cadets, accorded her a warm reception. NCC cadets also presented a Guard of Honour.

Speaking on the occasion, Minister Pi Lalrinpuii expressed happiness that Lunglei was hosting Mizoram's first NCC Fest and Parasailing Camp and thanked the organisers for bringing the event to south Mizoram. She said the programme reflected the Mizoram government's commitment towards balanced development across all regions of the state and highlighted the proposed establishment of Mizoram State University at Lunglei as one such initiative.

Referring to the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047, the Minister stressed the importance of empowering youth and described NCC as an ideal platform for nurturing disciplined, responsible and capable citizens. She encouraged cadets to equip themselves with modern knowledge and skills, adapt to changing global realities and contribute positively towards national integration and nation-building.

The programme also featured a felicitation ceremony honouring NCC alumni, individuals who rendered valuable services to NCC and youth development, and State achievers. The Minister presented awards and mementoes on behalf of NCC.

She later visited various stalls and interacted with exhibitors. The event also featured performances by the 23 Assam Rifles Pipe Band and cultural presentations by NCC cadets. A concert featuring prominent artistes is also scheduled in the evening.

The first-ever NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp in Mizoram is being held in Lunglei from May 5 to 11 to promote awareness about NCC and encourage youth participation. Cadets from different North Eastern states are taking part in the programme. The Parasailing Camp commenced on May 5 at 2nd Battalion MAP Ground, Luangmual, while the NCC Fest is being held on May 8 and 9 at C. Ṭhuamluaia Mual.

The Fest includes interactive sessions with senior government and military officials, meetings with NCC alumni, and a variety of exhibition stalls showcasing NCC activities, arms and equipment displays by the Border Security Force, Assam Rifles and Territorial Army Battalion, as well as stalls by the District Legal Services Authority and District Disaster Management Authority. Traditional Mizo handloom and handicraft products, along with a variety of Mizo delicacies, are also being showcased at the event.

title: HEALTH MINISTER PI LALRINPUII'N LUNGLEIAH NCC FEST & PARASAILING CAMP HMASA BER A HMANPUI
content:
446/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 9: Vawiin khan C. Ṭhuamluaia Mual, Lungleiah 2 Mizo Battalion National Cadet Corps (NCC) bul tuma Mizorama NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp hmasa ber kalpui mek chu Health & Family Welfare Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan Khuallian niin a hmanpui.

Minister hi Lunglei High Powered Committee (HPC) Vice Chairman Pu V. Malsawmtluanga, Lunglei Municipal Council (LMC) Vice Chairman Pu K. Lalrinawma leh District official-te'n an ṭawiawm a. 2 Mizo Bn NCC Commanding Officer Col. Sagar K. Mohanty hovin NCC hotute, sipai hotute leh NCC Cadet-te'n lo dawngsawngin, NCC Cadet-te'n Guard of Honour nen zahna chibai an lo buk.

Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan Mizorama NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp hmasa ber Lungleiin a dawlah a tel ve thei chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, a huaihawttute ṭhahnem ngaihna leh inpekna fakawm a tih thu a sawi a. Viksit Bharat 2047 hlawhtlin nan Mizoram sawrkar chuan mipui sawrkar, sawrkar ṭha leh lang tlang kalpuiin State chhunga hmasawnna rualkhai tak a awm theih nan theih tawp a chhuah tih sawiin, Lungleia Mizoram State University din tura ruahmanna leh hmun hrang hranga hmasawnna hna thawh kal mek te, Mizorama NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp hmasa ber chhim lama buatsaih te chu a nem nghettu a nih thu a sawi a. Viksit Bharat 2047 hlawhtlinna chu ṭhalaite, mi retheite, hmeichhiate leh kut hna thawktuteah a innghah thu sawiin, ram hmasawn nan ṭhalaite tihchak leh tihhmasawn a pawimawh thu leh NCC chu chumi atana hmanraw ṭha ber pakhat a nih thu a sawi a. Ṭhalaite chu ram hma hun innghahna an nih angin khawvel thiamna, inrelbawlna leh ei leh bar dinhmun danglam zel mila inher rem a, an thiamna leh theihna tuai hriam zel tur te, ram inpumkhatna vawng nung zel tur leh ram tana mi chhenfakawm nih tum zel tur te'n a chah.

He hunah hian NCC Alumni te, NCC leh ṭhalaite tana thawh hlawk tak takte leh ram pum huapa mi hming ṭhate chawimawina hun hman niin, Khuallianin NCC ai awhin chawimawina thilpek a hlan a. Khuallian hian stall hrang hrang fang kualin, stall nghaktute nen an inkawm a. 23 Assam Rifles Pipe Band te'n rimawi kal rem an entir bakah, NCC Cadet-te'n hnam lam an entir a. Tlai lamah zaithiam lar tak takte nen zai lam kutpui (concert) hun hman a ni bawk.

Mizorama NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp hmasa ber hi May ni 5–11 chhungin Lungleiah kalpui mek a ni a. NCC chungchang hriat zauna tur leh NCC zawm tura ṭhalaite fuih phurna tura ruahman niin, North East state hrang hrang aṭangin NCC cadet ṭhahnem tak an fuan khawm a. May ni 5 aṭangin 2nd Bn MAP Ground, Luangmualah Parasailing Camp hi kalpui ṭan niin, NCC Fest hi May ni 8 leh ni 9-ah C. Ṭhuamluaia Mualah nghah a ni thung.

NCC Fest-ah hian sawrkar hotu pawimawh leh sipai hotu liante nena inkawmna hun (interactive session) te, NCC chhuak (alumni)-te nena inkawmna hun te buatsaih niin, stall hrang hrang –NCC thil leh hriat tur pawimawh pho chhuahna, Border Security Force, Assam Rifles leh Territorial Army Bn te ralthuam pho chhuahna leh District Legal Services Authority leh District Disaster Management Authority stall te buatsaih a ni a. Mizoram tualchhuak puan leh themthiam thil mawi tak tak bakah, Mizo ei tur chi hrang hrangte chhawp chhuah a ni bawk.

title: MIZORAM POLICE THUCHHUAK: LUNGLEI DISTRICT JAIL CASE
content:
446/2026-2027

Mizoram Police Media Cell chuan a hnuaia mi ang hian May 9, 2026 hian thuchhuah an siam:-

Lunglei Police Station chuan Dt.27.04.2026 khan Pi R.Lalduhawmi, District & Sessions Judge, Lunglei Judicial District, Lunglei hnen atangin First Information Report (FIR) a dawng a. FIR-a a ziah danin Dt.24.04.2026 acourt-a a thut laiin tuna Lunglei District Jail a UTP Judicial Custody a awm mek mi pahnih Malsawmtluanga leh Dokapthanga te chu an inlan a. Heng mite hian Dt.13.04.2026-a Court of District & Sessions Judge, LungleiJudicial District lehkha leh Dt. 10.04.2026-a Hon’ble Guwahati High Court lehkha, Dt. 24.04.2026-a court hearing nei tur leh Rs 50,000 fine-a discharge bond-a an chhuahna tur order an rawn keng a. Mahse heng mi pahnihte hi a hma ni, Dt. 23.04.2026-ah ama hnenah ngei court hearing an nei zo chiah a, an hearing neih leh hun tur pawh Dt. 08.05.2026 ah fixed a lo ni tawh a ni.

He thil avang hian ringhlelin District Jail, Lungleiah thil awmzia chhui chiangtur leh clarification siam turin a kal nghal a. A chhui chianna aṭangin heng mi pahnih - Malsawmtluanga leh Dokapthanga te mai bakah hian lehkha dik lo hmanga jail tang (convict tawh) chhuah an awm nual nia a lan avangin Lunglei Police Station ah FIR a thehlut ta a ni.

Lunglei Police chuan rang taka hma la nghalin FIR in a sawi lan mi 15, document dik lo hmanga chhuah tawhte zawn hna hi an thawk nghal a, mi 11 chu man letin District Jail-ah dah let leh nghal an ni a, pakhat lo boral tawhin, Khawzawl Police-in an lo man mek pakhat awm bawkin, mi 13 te chinhriat nghal a ni. Chhui chhunzawm zelna atangin Document lem hmanga chhuah tak te hi mi 17 an lo ni zawk tih hriat a ni a, POCSO case te, ND&PS case leh ruk ruk case a convict tawh te an ni hlawm a ni.

Police in a chhui zelnaah he thila mawhphurtu ber nia lang, Jeremia Lalthangtura (25) s/o James Lalzawmmawia of Zemabawk, Berawlui veng, Aizawl tuna Dictrict Jail Lunglei a UTP a tang mek leh C.Laltlanhlua (31) s/o C.Lalbiaksanga of Republic Vengthlang, Aizawl, tuna Dictrict Jail, Lungleia a nghet lova ambulance driver hna thawk mek te chu an inhnamhnawih ngei nia hriain, Dt.01.05.2026 khan man an ni a,Dt.7.05.2026 khan District Jail, Lungleiah dah luh an ni.

Investigation atanga a lan danin Jeremia Lalthangtura hian heng convict mi 17 te hi lo bum kualin an case-ah appeal a siam sak theih thu leh a hmelhriat Ukil a lo biaksak tur thu tiamin pawisa a lo laksak thin a. Tanghohnen atang hian Jeremia hian cheng sangli (Rs.4,000/-) atanga cheng singnga(Rs.50,000/-) thlengin pawisa hi a lo laksak thin a, heng atanga pawisa an hmuh zawng zawng hi Rs.1,35,000/- bawr vel a ni. An pawisa hmuh te hi Jeremia leh C.Laltlanhlua te hian an lo insem thin tih hriatchhuah a ni.

Pawisa hi C.Laltlanhlua kutah emaw a Gpay account-ah a lut thin a. C.Laltlanhlua phone leh banking transaction zawng zawng hi a remtihna thlapin Jeremia Lalthangtura hian Jail chhunga atang hian a hmansak thina ni.

Document lem te hi Jeremia Lalthangtura hian hmun hnih, Rahsi venga Xerox leh Printing tih theihna Computer dawr pakhat leh District Jail, Lunglei-a office computer-ah te a lo siam thin tih finfiah a ni a. C.Laltlanhlua hian office chahbi te lo dil chhuakin Jeremia-a'n computer a hman lai hian a lo veng thin tih hriat a ni. Heng a document siam chawp te hi C.Laltlanhluahnenah hlanin C.Laltlanhlua hian Jail thuneitute hnenah a pe thin a ni. Hengdocument lem siamna atana hman computer leh printer te hi man niin,Forensic Science Laboratory-ah thawn nghal a ni bawk.

Jeremia Lalthangtura hian jail tangte hi bumin an chhuah theihna turin hma a la a, pawisa an chawi hnuah an chhuahna order a lo thlen mai tur thu hrilhin, heng order te hi amahin siam chawpin C.Laltlanhlua hian alo pui thin a ni. Heng document leh Court order lem te hi Jail thuneitute hnena an thlen hnuah jail thuneitute hian order hmang hian jail tangte an lo chhuah ve mai thin a ni. Heng mi tang 17, document lem hmanga chhuahtehi Dt.30.01.2026 leh Dt.18.03.2026 inkarah Jail aṭanga chhuah an ni.He case hi Investigation la zawh loh niin chhui chhunzawm mek zel a ni e.

title: COOPERATIVE SOCIETIES OF NORTH EAST INDIA HOLD WORKSHOP UNDER THE THEME “SAHKAR SE SAMRIDDHI”
content:
65/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, 8 May 2026:A meeting of Cooperation Secretaries and Ministry of Cooperation in collaboration with NABARD and Cooperation Department, Government of Mizoram organised a Regional Workshop for North Eastern States on Cooperatives at Conference Hall of Assembly House Annexue, Aizawl. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Dr. Ashish Kumar Butani, Secretary, Ministry of Cooperation, Government of India.

Welcoming the participants, Mr. Udit Prakash Rai, Commissioner & Secretary of the Cooperation Department, Government of Mizoram, stated that such a gathering would greatly help Cooperative Societies in the North Eastern states to continue progressing. He highlighted that through various initiatives such as regional economic transformation, digital empowerment, and “Sahkar Se Samriddhi” (Prosperity through Cooperation), development could be brought to both rural and urban areas.

A keynote address was delivered online by Dr. Shaji K.V., Chairman, NABARD in the inaugural session.He addressed the gathering and emphasized the important role played by Cooperative Societies and NABARD in ensuring inclusive and sustainable development in the North Eastern region. He stressed the need for Cooperative Societies to work actively in Sixth Schedule areas and remote districts to address local challenges effectively. He further stated that other states should learn from the successful experiences of Assam and Tripura.

Speaking on the initiatives undertaken by Cooperative Societies and NABARD, Mr. Shaji K. V. also mentioned that the Mizoram State Cooperative Bank has attained Scheduled Bank status and that the MPACS computerization programme is currently being implemented. He assured that technical and handholding support from NABARD would continue and expressed confidence that these efforts would bring balanced development to the North Eastern states.

The workshop focused on several key themes aimed at enhancing the cooperative ecosystem in the region. Discussions were held on strengthening value chains and market linkages through the role of national cooperative entities such as NCEL, NCOL, and BBSSL. Participants also deliberated on cooperative banking initiatives and the expectations from Northeastern States in expanding cooperative credit and financial inclusion.

A major highlight of the workshop was the detailed discussion on the World’s Largest Grain Storage Plan (WLGSP). The session focused on strategies for achieving storage targets, monitoring loan sanctioning processes under AMI, ensuring effective implementation frameworks for PACS, and improving financial viability and coordination among agencies. Presentations were made by NABARD regional offices, state representatives, and national institutions including FCI, NAFED, NCCF, CWC/SWC, and WDRA.

The workshop also featured a session by NEDFi on strategies for the development of cooperatives in the Northeastern Region, including the successful journey of Konoklota Mahila Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Jorhat, Assam.

During the session on innovative models and field-level successes, representatives from Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Nagaland, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Tripura, and Sikkim shared state-specific initiatives, best practices, policy responses, and growth strategies to strengthen cooperative institutions and improve market access for local products. Another important session focused on the formation and strengthening of cooperatives such as MPACS, MDCS, and other business affiliations.

The workshop concluded with a session on strengthening state federations and outlining the way forward for cooperative development in the region. The concluding session included addresses by Shri Khili Ram Meena, Chief Secretary, Government of Mizoram, and Dr. Ashish Kumar Bhutani, Secretary, Ministry of Cooperation, Government of India.

The event reaffirmed the collective commitment of the Government of India, state governments, NABARD, and partner institutions towards promoting cooperative-led development and ensuring inclusive growth and prosperity across the Northeastern States under the vision of “Sahkar se Samriddhi”.

title: 'NEONATAL RESUSCITATION PROGRAM DAY' HMANG
content:
445/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl the 9th May, 2026 :Vawiin khan Zoram Medical College & Hospital a Department of Pediatrics leh Department of Obstetrics & Gynaecology te tangkawp chuan 'Neonatal Resuscitation Program Day' an hmang a. ZMC& H Dean, Professor Swati M Patki in a hmanpui.

He program hi Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) leh National Neonatology Forum (NNF) bul tum, ram pum huap state 35 leh UT tinah a ruala hman ho tura duan niin, nausen pianghlim harsatna nei te chhanchhuah dan training hrang hrang Nurse leh doctor tam thei ang ber te hnenah pek a ni.  India ramah hian nausen piang 10 zelah 1 hi thawk lama harsatna neiin an lo piang thin a, a hun taka buaipui nghal anih chuan nausen tam tak chhanchhuah an lo ni ta thin a ni.

He training ah hian naupang doctor, gynaecologist, medical officers, interns leh nurse thahnem tak train anni a, nausen pianghlim harsatna neite chhanchhuah an lo nih zel theih nan leh Mizoram in Infant mortality rate (IMR) hniam a neih zel theih nana hmalakna pawimawh tak a ni dawn a ni.

Attachment

title: SAHKAR SE SAMRIDDHI KHARNA HUN NEI
content:
444/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 8th May, 2026: India hmarchhak a Cooperative Societies te tana workshop (zir hona), ‘Sahkar se Samriddhi’ (Thawh hona hmanga hlawhtlinna) chu nimin khan Assembly House a Conference Hall-ah a kharna hun hman a ni. He zir hona hi Ministry of Cooperation (Government of India), NABARD leh Cooperation Department (Government of Mizoram) buatsaih a ni a, India hmarchhaka Cooperation Secretary-te leh Cooperative Societies a Registrars te an tel a ni.

Ministry of Cooperation Secretary Dr. Ashish Kumar Bhutani chuan north-east region in harsatna a tawh bikte hre chung zela hma lak a pawimawh thu a sawi a, state tin atanga kalkhawmte chu an hmalakna kawng hrang hrangah National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC) tanpuina hmang tangkai turin a fuih. India rama cooperative bank te pui turin sorkar laipui chuan Sahakar Sarathi (rural area tan) leh National Urban Cooperative Finance and Development Corporation (NUCFDC) (urban area tan) a awm tih a sawi bawk.

Chief Secretary Pu Khiili Ram Meena pawhin thu sawiin cooperative sector chuan Mizote nunah nasa takin hna a thawk a. Mizoram chu India rama a hmasa pawl ni turin Registrar Society Office chu a computerize vek tawh a ni a, Primary Agriculture Cooperative Credit Societies (PACS) 24 te pawh computerize tawh niin, 75 dang te chu computerize mek an ni. Mizoram sawrkar leh cooperative sector te chuan Bana Kaih hmangin kawng hrang hrangin hna an thawk ho tih a sawi bawk

Pu Udit Prakash Rai, Cooperation Department Commissioner leh Secretary chuan lawmthu sawina in he hun hi a khar a ni.

Attachment
